name: Razorpay Payment Analytics Engine
scenario: Razorpay's analytics API handles 1M range-sum queries per day on static payment data. Current O(n) per query system is too slow. Build a prefix-sum powered analytics engine with O(1) per query.

core.description: RangeQueryEngine: buildPrefix(int[] payments) → int[] prefix (n+1 size). rangeSum(prefix, l, r) → O(1). Test: rangeSum on [100,250,180,320,150] for range [1,3] = 750.
subarraySumEqualsK(int[] payments, int k): count subarrays summing to k. prefixCount.put(0,1) + HashMap. Test: [1,1,1] k=2 → 2.
productExceptSelf(int[] nums): O(n) time O(1) extra space. Left pass then right pass. Test: [1,2,3,4] → [24,12,8,6].
pivotIndex(int[] nums): leftSum == rightSum. O(n) O(1). Test: [1,7,3,6,5,6] → 3.
@SpringBootTest (4 tests): rangeSum correct, subarraySum=2, product=[24,12,8,6], pivot=3.
README: explains why sliding window fails for negative payment arrays.

bonus.description: MatrixRangeSum: 2D prefix sum. O(mn) build, O(1) query. Test: match LeetCode #304 examples.
continuousSubarrayDivisibleByK: subarrays divisible by k. Prefix mod HashMap. Test: [23,2,4,6,7] k=6 → true.

ai_policy_explanation: 5 hints. Prefix array size n+1: prefix[0]=0, prefix[i]=prefix[i-1]+nums[i-1]. rangeSum formula: prefix[r+1]-prefix[l] (NOT prefix[r]-prefix[l-1] which crashes for l=0). Sliding window with negatives: FAILS — use prefix+HashMap. SubarraySumK: prefixCount.put(0,1) handles subarrays starting at index 0. If sum-k was seen before: those many subarrays end here summing to k. ProductExceptSelf: result[i] = product of all left × product of all right. Left pass fills result with prefix products. Right pass multiplies by running suffix product (rightProduct).
